title: Continuation of the National Emergency With Respect to South Sudan
summary: A notice from the White House dated March 24, 2026, announces the continuation of the national emergency concerning South Sudan, which was initially declared on April 3, 2014, via Executive Order 13664. This emergency is due to the ongoing threat to U.S. national security and foreign policy posed by the situation in South Sudan, characterized by violence, human rights abuses, child soldier recruitment, attacks on peacekeepers, and obstruction of humanitarian aid. The national emergency will remain in effect for another year, as per the National Emergencies Act.
